evidences.title: ET to UTC Converter - Time.now
evidences.content: Time Difference: UTC is 5 hours ahead of EST (winter) and 4 hours ahead of EDT (summer). The difference changes twice yearly with daylight saving transitions. #### Daylight Saving Time Impact Winter Period (EST): From first Sunday in November to second Sunday in March, ET is UTC-5, making UTC 5 hours ahead Summer Period (EDT): From second Sunday in March to first Sunday in November, ET is UTC-4, making UTC 4 hours ahead UTC Stability: UTC never changes and remains constant year-round, making it ideal for international coordination and technical applications #### Common Conversion Examples Business Hours (Winter) EST 9:00 AM → UTC 2:00 PM EST 5:00 PM → UTC 10:00 PM 5-hour difference during winter Business Hours (Summer) EDT 9:00 AM → UTC 1:00 PM EDT 5:00 PM → UTC 9:00 PM [...] EDT 9:00 AM → UTC 1:00 PM EDT 5:00 PM → UTC 9:00 PM 4-hour difference during summer International Coordination EST Midnight → UTC 5:00 AM EDT Midnight → UTC 4:00 AM UTC used for global scheduling Technical Applications Server logs → UTC timestamps API calls → UTC standards UTC preferred for technical systems #### Conversion Tips & Best Practices • Add 5 hours to EST (winter) or 4 hours to EDT (summer) to ge...

evidences.content: # EDT to UTC Converter EDT to UTC Converter - Available at: - Accessed: March 26, 2026. #### Need a Different Conversion? ## Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) ## Conversion Settings ## Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) ### EDT to UTC Conversion Guide #### What is EDT to UTC Conversion? EDT to UTC conversion helps you translate time between Eastern Daylight Time and Coordinated Universal Time. EDT is UTC-4 and is observed during daylight saving time from March to November. UTC is the world time standard and remains constant year-round. The time difference is always 4 hours, with UTC being ahead of EDT. #### Time Zone Information #### Daylight Saving Time Impact #### Common Conversion Examples #### Conversion Tips & Best Practices ## Convert Time Between EDT and UTC [...] ### Step 1: Pick Your Starting Time Select a date and time using the inputs labeled under "Eastern Daylight Time (EDT)" or "Coordinated Universal Time (UTC)," depending on which direction you’re converting. ### Step 2: Choose the Time Zone You’re Starting From Use the dropdown to confirm if you’re entering an EDT or UTC time. evidences.title: EST to UTC - Online Converter
evidences.content: Based International Air Transport Association (IATA) timezone codes. Time zones use hours and minutes offset from Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), most a whole number of hours, a few are offset by 30 or 45 minutes. Eastern Standard Time (EST) is UTC-5:00, and Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) is UTC-4:00, this time zone is called Eastern Time Zone (ET) in the United States, parts of eastern Canada, Mexico, Panama and the Caribbean Islands. Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) is the primary time standard now, time zones around the world are expressed using offsets from UTC, UTC offset is the difference in hours and minutes from UTC, a time zone can be determined by adding or subtracting the number of UTC offset. [...] OnlineConverter.com # EST to UTC Eastern Standard Time (EST) Date Time ## Calculation Results | | | --- | | Time Zone | Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) | | Time | ## Introduction This free tool can help you convert Eastern Standard Time (EST) to Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). You just need to select the data and time of the source time zone and the tool will calculate the date and time for the destination time zone. ## How To Use Select a date by using the "Date" option...

EST is 5 hours behind of UTC. If you are in EST, the most convenient time to accommodate all parties is between 9:00 am and 1:00 pm for a conference call or meeting. In UTC, this will be a usual working time of between 2:00 pm and 6:00 pm.
